items.text: Essence, determinants, and consequences of Ethics in human actions; dimensions of ethics
Ethics in private and public relationships; Values and Ethics in government
Human Values: Lessons from the lives and teachings of great leaders, reformers, and administrators
Role of family, society, and educational institutions in inculcating values
Attitude: Content, structure, function; its influence and relation with thought and behaviour
Moral and political attitudes; social influence and persuasion tactics
Emotional Intelligence: Concepts, utility, and application in administration and governance
EI Competencies: Self-awareness cluster, self-management cluster, social awareness, relationship management
Aptitude and foundational values for Civil Service (Integrity, Impartiality, Non-partisanship, Objectivity)
Dedication to public service, empathy, tolerance, and compassion towards the weaker sections
Civil Services Accountability, neutrality, anonymity, and professional ethics
Contributions of moral thinkers and philosophers from India (Mahatma Gandhi, Radhakrishnan, Tagore, Vivekananda, Ambedkar)
Contributions of moral thinkers from the World (Socrates, Plato, Aristotle, Kant, Mill, Locke, Rousseau, Marx)
Concept of public service; Philosophical basis of governance and probity
Information sharing and transparency in government, Right to Information (RTI) act and flaws
Codes of Ethics, Codes of Conduct, Citizen's Charters, and Work Culture
Challenges of Corruption (extents, dimensions, response, whistleblowers, public funds utilization)
Applied Ethics: Euthanasia, Surrogacy, Environmental ethics, Business/Corporate ethics
Media & Sports Ethics: Digital Media, Paid News, Doping, sportsmanship
Case Studies on all the above areas (Solving administrative ethical dilemmas, public interest decisions)
